message:
fix(qjsc): add -e flag to execute top-level code in bytecode

Without the -e flag, qjsc produces bytecode that only defines the
module but doesn't execute top-level code when evaluated with
JS_EvalFunction(). This caused console.log and other top-level
statements to silently not run.

The -e flag tells qjsc to generate bytecode that executes top-level
code when the bytecode is evaluated, which is the expected behavior.
